content: Antelope Valley College general-education planning for Fall 2025 and later uses Cal-GETC, the unified California General Education Transfer Curriculum. The published certification data contains these areas: Area 1A English Composition; Area 1B Critical Thinking and Composition; Area 1C Oral Communication; Area 2 Mathematical Concepts and Quantitative Reasoning; Area 3A Arts; Area 3B Humanities; Area 4 Social and Behavioral Sciences; Area 5A Physical Science; Area 5B Biological Science; Area 5C Laboratory; Area 6 Ethnic Studies. A transfer plan must consider Cal-GETC in addition to ASSIST major preparation. The area records contain the AVC-certified options. Do not assume that one course can satisfy multiple areas, or that completing one course in every listed code is by itself a certification; verify the official completion, double-counting, campus, and local graduation rules with an AVC counselor.

Cal-GETC Area 1A: English Composition. AVC-certified course options for 2025-26 are: ENGLC1000 (Academic Reading and Writing, 3 units); ENGLC1000H (Academic Reading and Writing - Honors, 3 units). These are alternatives certified for this area, not a direction to take every listed course. Confirm selection and all Cal-GETC completion rules with an AVC counselor.

Cal-GETC Area 1B: Critical Thinking and Composition. AVC-certified course options for 2025-26 are: PHIL201 (Critical Thinking, 3 units); ENGLC1001 (Critical Thinking and Writing, 3 units); ENGLC1001H (Critical Thinking and Writing - Honors, 3 units). These are alternatives certified for this area, not a direction to take every listed course. Confirm selection and all Cal-GETC completion rules with an AVC counselor.
